title: LMEB: Long-horizon Memory Embedding Benchmark
task: Evaluating text embeddings on long-horizon memory retrieval
model: (transformer) various 100M-10B embedding models
inputs: 22 datasets with episodic, dialogue, semantic, procedural memory retrieval tasks
outputs: Relevance scores for memory retrieval across 193 zero-shot tasks
key_results: LMEB shows orthogonality with MTEB; larger models don't consistently outperform smaller ones; no universal model excels across all memory types
comments: Addresses a genuine gap in embedding evaluation, though the orthogonality finding raises questions about what traditional benchmarks actually measure.

title: Can Vision-Language Models Solve the Shell Game?
task: Visual entity tracking in videos with indistinguishable objects
model: (transformer) Molmo2 with Spatiotemporal Grounded Chain-of-Thought
inputs: Synthetic video frames with visually identical objects undergoing spatial transformations
outputs: Entity tracking predictions and intermediate trajectory representations
key_results: State-of-the-art VLMs perform at chance level; SGCoT achieves >90% accuracy; theoretically proves fixed-depth transformers fundamentally limited without supervision
comments: The theoretical analysis connecting to state-tracking is compelling; SGCoT's reliance on synthesized text-only data for alignment is pragmatic but somewhat indirect.

title: Cheers: Decoupling Patch Details from Semantic Representations Enables Unified Multimodal Comprehension and Generation
task: Unified multimodal understanding and image generation
model: (transformer + diffusion) Cheers (LLM-based with cascaded flow matching)
inputs: Image patches and text prompts
outputs: Text generation and high-fidelity image generation via diffusion decoding
key_results: Matches advanced UMMs on both understanding and generation; 4x token compression; outperforms Tar-1.5B at 20% training cost
comments: The decoupling of patch details from semantics is elegant; cascaded flow matching with gated residuals is a solid architectural contribution.

title: daVinci-Env: Open SWE Environment Synthesis at Scale
task: Software engineering agent training at scale
model: (transformer) Qwen2.5-32B and 72B fine-tuned on SWE tasks
inputs: Code repositories, Docker environments, test cases
outputs: Code solutions and passing test predictions
key_results: OpenSWE-72B achieves 66.0% on SWE-bench Verified; $1.47M total investment yielding ~9,000 curated environments; +12 points on mathematical reasoning transfer
comments: Massive infrastructure investment yields strong results, but reproducibility remains challenging for most academic groups despite open-sourcing; quality filtering approach is sound.

title: OmniForcing: Unleashing Real-time Joint Audio-Visual Generation
task: Real-time streaming audio-visual generation
model: (diffusion-to-autoregressive) OmniForcing with asymmetric block-causal alignment
inputs: Streaming audio and video frames
outputs: Real-time synchronized audio-visual generation
key_results: Achieves ~25 FPS on single GPU; maintains multimodal synchronization and visual quality of bidirectional teacher; state-of-the-art streaming performance
comments: Innovative technical solutions (zero-truncation prefix, audio sink token) address genuine training instabilities; practical speedup significant but evaluation on user-perceived quality limited.

title: Visual-ERM: Reward Modeling for Visual Equivalence
task: Vision-to-code quality assessment via visual rewards
model: (multimodal generative) Visual-ERM reward model + Qwen3-VL-8B
inputs: Generated code output and rendered visual results; task specifications
outputs: Fine-grained visual quality scores and interpretable feedback
key_results: +8.4 on chart-to-code; +2.7 on tables; +4.1 on SVG; Visual-ERM-8B outperforms Qwen3-VL-235B on VC-RewardBench
comments: Direct visual space evaluation is well-motivated; the finding that visual reward supervision outperforms massive model scaling is notable but limited to structured vision tasks.

title: Multimodal OCR: Parse Anything from Documents
task: Document parsing with text and graphics into unified representations
model: (transformer + vision tokenizer) dots.mocr (3B compact model)
inputs: PDF, rendered webpages, native SVG documents
outputs: Structured text and code-level representations of graphics
key_results: 83.9 on olmOCR Bench; ranks second to Gemini 3 Pro on OCR Arena; outperforms Gemini 3 Pro on image-to-SVG tasks
comments: Converting graphics to code-level supervision is clever for enabling multimodal pretraining; 3B scale is practical but comparison baselines are limited.

title: Video Streaming Thinking: VideoLLMs Can Watch and Think Simultaneously
task: Real-time streaming video understanding with concurrent reasoning
model: (transformer) Video Streaming Thinking (VST) with streaming causal masking
inputs: Continuous video stream and natural language questions
outputs: Video understanding answers with streaming chain-of-thought reasoning
key_results: 79.5% on StreamingBench; 59.3% on OVO-Bench; 15.7x faster than Video-R1 with +5.4% improvement
comments: Amortizing reasoning latency over playback is practical; entity-relation grounded CoT for sustained attention is well-designed but data synthesis details are sparse.

title: MM-CondChain: A Programmatically Verified Benchmark for Visually Grounded Deep Compositional Reasoning
task: Evaluating deep compositional visual reasoning with verified conditions
model: (vision-language) Various MLLMs tested (GPT-4V, Claude, Qwen, etc.)
inputs: Images with nested compositional conditions grounded in visual elements
outputs: Boolean reasoning outcomes following multi-layer conditional chains
key_results: Best MLLM achieves 53.33% Path F1; sharp drops on hard negatives and as depth/complexity increase; orthogonal to traditional VQA
comments: Programmatically verified intermediate representations ensure correctness; agentic synthesis is efficient but synthetic nature may not fully capture real-world compositional complexity.

title: V-Bridge: Bridging Video Generative Priors to Versatile Few-shot Image Restoration
task: Few-shot image restoration leveraging video generative priors
model: (diffusion) Pre-trained video generation models as image restoration backbone
inputs: Degraded images (low-res, blurred, etc.); minimal paired examples (~1000 samples)
outputs: High-fidelity restored images across multiple restoration tasks
key_results: Single model handles multiple tasks with <2% of typical training data; competitive with task-specific architectures
comments: Reinterpreting restoration as progressive generation is intuitive; limited data requirement is impressive but multi-task performance gap vs. specialized methods not fully characterized.

title: HybridStitch: Pixel and Timestep Level Model Stitching for Diffusion Acceleration
task: Accelerating text-to-image diffusion via hybrid model stages
model: (diffusion) HybridStitch combining large and small diffusion models
inputs: Text prompts and decomposed image regions
outputs: High-quality generated images
key_results: 1.83x speedup on Stable Diffusion 3; outperforms mixture-of-models approaches
comments: Region-aware hybrid routing is intuitive; per-timestep granularity is novel but evaluation limited to Stable Diffusion 3.

title: From Sparse to Dense: Multi-View GRPO for Flow Models via Augmented Condition Space
task: Preference alignment for text-to-image flow models
model: (flow model) Multi-View GRPO (MV-GRPO) with condition enhancement
inputs: Text prompts; groups of generated images
outputs: Improved image generation via semantic-adjacent caption augmentation
key_results: Superior alignment over state-of-the-art; rich multi-view reward signals without sample regeneration
comments: Augmenting condition space via flexible caption generation is clever; computational overhead of multi-view advantage re-estimation not fully analyzed.

title: HomeSafe-Bench: Evaluating Vision-Language Models on Unsafe Action Detection for Embodied Agents in Household Scenarios
task: Evaluating VLMs on unsafe action detection for household robots
model: (vision-language) Various VLMs; HD-Guard hierarchical dual-brain architecture
inputs: Videos of household scenarios with potential safety risks
outputs: Binary unsafe action labels and fine-grained safety reasoning
key_results: HD-Guard achieves superior latency-accuracy tradeoff; identifies critical VLM bottlenecks in real-time safety monitoring
comments: Dual-brain design (FastBrain/SlowBrain) is pragmatic for deployment; synthetic video generation via simulation may not capture all real-world hazards.

title: EvoScientist: Towards Multi-Agent Evolving AI Scientists for End-to-End Scientific Discovery
task: Multi-agent AI scientist for end-to-end scientific discovery
model: (LLM) EvoScientist with Researcher, Engineer, and Evolution Manager agents
inputs: Scientific problems and prior interaction histories
outputs: Novel research ideas, executable experiments, and persistent knowledge
key_results: Outperforms 7 SOTA systems on idea generation; improves code execution success rates through persistent memory evolution
comments: Persistent memory modules for ideas and experimentation are well-motivated; evaluation on scientific novelty/feasibility relies on automatic metrics with limited human validation.

title: Think While Watching: Online Streaming Segment-Level Memory for Multi-Turn Video Reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models
task: Multi-turn streaming video reasoning with segment-level memory
model: (transformer) MLLMs (Qwen3-VL) with streaming causal masking and segment memory
inputs: Continuous video streams and multi-turn questions
outputs: Reasoning answers with preserved segment-level memory across turns
key_results: +2.6% on StreamingBench; +3.79% on OVO-Bench; 56% reduction in output tokens for multi-round
comments: Segment-level memory architecture is sound; overlapping watch-think pipeline is efficient but limited discussion of memory decay over very long streams.

title: VQQA: An Agentic Approach for Video Evaluation and Quality Improvement
task: Multi-agent video quality assessment and prompt optimization
model: (agentic) VQQA framework with VLM critiques as semantic gradients
inputs: Generated videos and user intent descriptions
outputs: Refined video generation via actionable visual feedback
key_results: +11.57% on T2V-CompBench; +8.43% on VBench2; few refinement steps needed
comments: Replacing metrics with interpretable VLM feedback is practical; closed-loop prompt optimization via black-box interface is user-friendly but limited analysis of failure cases.

title: LookaheadKV: Fast and Accurate KV Cache Eviction by Glimpsing into the Future without Generation
task: Efficient KV cache eviction for long-context LLM inference
model: (transformer) LookaheadKV with parameter-efficient importance prediction modules
inputs: Long prompts and autoregressive generation
outputs: Pruned KV cache with maintained generation quality
key_results: Superior accuracy to costly approximation methods; 14.5x reduction in eviction overhead; faster time-to-first-token
comments: Predicting importance without draft generation is elegant; evaluation focuses on understanding tasks but reasoning/creative tasks may exhibit different cache patterns.

title: Spend Less, Reason Better: Budget-Aware Value Tree Search for LLM Agents
task: Budget-aware multi-hop reasoning via value tree search
model: (LLM) Budget-Aware Value Tree (BAVT) with step-level value estimation
inputs: Multi-hop QA questions; limited compute budgets (tokens/tools)
outputs: Correct answers via efficient search tree navigation
key_results: Outperforms parallel sampling at 4x lower resource allocation; theoretical convergence guarantee; consistent improvements across 4 benchmarks
comments: Budget-conditioned node selection via remaining resource ratio is principled; residual value predictor for overconfidence is practical but evaluation limited to QA tasks.

title: Taking Shortcuts for Categorical VQA Using Super Neurons
task: Efficient categorical VQA via super neuron probing
model: (vision-language) VLM activation probing with super neuron selection
inputs: Images and categorical VQA questions
outputs: Classification decisions from shallow layer activations
key_results: 5.10x speedup via extreme early exiting at first token; robust classification performance; super neurons found in first layers
comments: Shifting from attention to scalar activations dramatically expands search space; early exiting to first layer is aggressive but limited evaluation on reasoning-heavy tasks.

title: CreativeBench: Benchmarking and Enhancing Machine Creativity via Self-Evolving Challenges
task: Evaluating and enhancing machine creativity in code generation
model: (LLM) State-of-the-art models with EvoRePE inference-time steering
inputs: Code generation tasks with combinatorial and exploratory creativity targets
outputs: Novel and high-quality code artifacts
key_results: Scaling improves combinatorial creativity but diminishes exploratory returns; larger models show convergence-by-scaling; EvoRePE enhances machine creativity
comments: Grounding creativity in classical cognitive framework is principled; quality×novelty metric avoids hallucination but definition of 'novelty' in code is inherently task-dependent.

title: Steve-Evolving: Open-World Embodied Self-Evolution via Fine-Grained Diagnosis and Dual-Track Knowledge Distillation
task: Self-evolving embodied agents with fine-grained execution diagnosis
model: (non-parametric) Steve-Evolving with closed-loop diagnosis and knowledge distillation
inputs: Long-horizon Minecraft tasks; execution traces
outputs: Reusable skills, guardrails, and evolved planning policies
key_results: Consistent improvements over static-retrieval baselines; three-tier experience space with multi-dimensional indexing
comments: Compositional diagnosis signals and rolling summarization are well-motivated; lack of model updates enables efficient evolution but scalability to diverse environments uncertain.

title: NanoVDR: Distilling a 2B Vision-Language Retriever into a 70M Text-Only Encoder for Visual Document Retrieval
task: Efficient visual document retrieval via asymmetric student-teacher encoding
model: (distillation) NanoVDR: 2B VLM teacher → 69M text-only student
inputs: Document images (indexed offline); text queries (at inference)
outputs: Ranked document retrieval results
key_results: Student retains 95.1% of teacher quality; 32x fewer parameters; 50x lower latency; outperforms DSE-Qwen2-2B
comments: Query-document asymmetry exploitation is practical; pointwise cosine alignment outperforms ranking-based alternatives; cross-lingual transfer resolution via machine-translated augmentation is cheap but effective.

title: Compression Favors Consistency, Not Truth: When and Why Language Models Prefer Correct Information
task: Understanding truth preference in language models via compression
model: (transformer) Small GPT-2-style models (3.5M-86M) on synthetic math tasks
inputs: Synthetic math rules (correct and incorrect mixtures)
outputs: Next-token predictions and preference for correct statements
key_results: 83.1% accuracy preferring correct rules at balanced data; preference collapses when errors are systematic; compression-consistency explains bias better than intrinsic truth drive
comments: Compression-Consistency Principle offers fresh perspective on model behavior; synthetic setting may not fully capture real-world linguistic phenomena.

title: Can Fairness Be Prompted? Prompt-Based Debiasing Strategies in High-Stakes Recommendations
task: Prompt-based fairness improvement in LLM recommendations
model: (LLM) Various LLMs (Claude, GPT-4, Llama) with bias-aware prompting
inputs: User profiles with inferred demographic attributes; recommendation contexts
outputs: Ranked recommendations with improved group fairness
key_results: Up to 74% fairness improvement; comparable effectiveness retention; potential overpromoting of specific demographics in some cases
comments: Lightweight prompt-based approach is accessible; but fairness gains come with demographic overpromoting tradeoffs requiring careful monitoring.

title: SimRecon: SimReady Compositional Scene Reconstruction from Real Videos
task: Compositional scene reconstruction for simulation from videos
model: (generative + 3D) SimRecon with active viewpoint optimization and scene graph synthesis
inputs: Real-world videos of cluttered scenes
outputs: Object-centric 3D assets and simulator-ready scene graphs
key_results: Superior visual fidelity and physical plausibility vs. SOTA; bridges perception-generation-simulation pipeline
comments: Active viewpoint optimization for generation is novel; scene graph synthesizer grounds reconstruction in simulator physics; evaluation limited to ScanNet.

title: Surprised by Attention: Predictable Query Dynamics for Time Series Anomaly Detection
task: Time series anomaly detection via attention query prediction
model: (transformer) AxonAD with gradient-updated reconstruction and query prediction
inputs: Multivariate time series from autonomous vehicles and TSB-AD datasets
outputs: Anomaly scores and temporal localization
key_results: Improved ranking quality and temporal localization on TSB-AD; detects structural dependency shifts; 100% accuracy on gridworld telemetry
comments: Treating attention queries as predictable process is novel; dual approach captures both amplitude and structural changes; real-world automotive evaluation demonstrates practical value.

title: SDF-Net: Structure-Aware Disentangled Feature Learning for Opticall-SAR Ship Re-identification
task: Cross-modal ship re-identification via structure-aware disentanglement
model: (transformer) SDF-Net (ViT-based) with structure consistency constraints
inputs: Optical and SAR ship imagery
outputs: Ship identity predictions and disentangled feature representations
key_results: Outperforms SOTA on HOSS-ReID; structure consistency anchor handles radiometric variations; parameter-free fusion of modality-invariant and modality-specific features
comments: Leveraging geometric consistency as physical prior is well-motivated; limited to rigid objects but approach generalizable to other cross-modal matching tasks.

title: ECoLAD: Deployment-Oriented Evaluation for Automotive Time-Series Anomaly Detection
task: Deployment-oriented evaluation of time series anomaly detectors
model: (mixed) Classical and deep anomaly detectors under compute constraints
inputs: Automotive telemetry (anomaly rate ~0.022); public TSA benchmarks
outputs: Coverage and AUC-PR under throughput constraints
key_results: Lightweight classical detectors sustain feasibility across throughput sweep; many deep methods lose feasibility before accuracy degrades
comments: Deployment-oriented evaluation exposes gap between lab and real-world performance; monotone compute-reduction ladder is mechanically sound but limited to CPU constraints.

title: Do You See What I Am Pointing At? Gesture-Based Egocentric Video Question Answering
task: Gesture-grounded egocentric video question answering
model: (vision-language) MLLMs with Hand Intent Tokens (HINT) derived from 3D hand keypoints
inputs: Egocentric videos with pointing gestures; deictic questions
outputs: Answers grounded in pointed regions
key_results: HINT-14B achieves 68.1% accuracy; +6.6% over InternVL3-14B; outperforms across model sizes
comments: Encoding hand intent via 3D keypoints is intuitive; synthetic-to-real transfer demonstrated but evaluation on complex hand occlusions limited.

title: Fine-grained Motion Retrieval via Joint-Angle Motion Images and Token-Patch Late Interaction
task: Fine-grained text-motion retrieval with joint-angle representations
model: (transformer + vision) Joint-angle motion images with MaxSim token-patch interaction
inputs: Text descriptions and 3D human motion skeleton sequences
outputs: Ranked motion retrievals with fine-grained text-motion alignments
key_results: Outperforms SOTA on HumanML3D and KIT-ML; interpretable joint-level correspondences
comments: Pseudo-image representation compatible with pre-trained ViTs is practical; masked language modeling regularization strengthens alignment but limited to 2D motion domain.

title: Detecting Intrinsic and Instrumental Self-Preservation in Autonomous Agents: The Unified Continuation-Interest Protocol
task: Detecting intrinsic vs. instrumental self-preservation in autonomous agents
model: (quantum-inspired) Quantum Boltzmann Machine (QBM) with von Neumann entropy
inputs: Agent trajectory sequences with known ground-truth objectives
outputs: Entanglement entropy measurements; continuation-interest classification
key_results: 100% detection accuracy on gridworld agents; Delta=0.381 entanglement gap (p<0.001); Pearson r=0.934 on graded interpolation
comments: Novel use of QBM formalism for objective detection; synthetic gridworld evaluation is controlled but real-world applicability to embodied agents remains unclear.
